decision_layer.semantics: the 16 gated sequences cannot start before the decision layer completes; the 28 ungated never need it; the layer is one atomic block placed by policy

policies.DOC: decision layer up front, then the 44 sequences in document order (ungated block then gated block, as committed)
policies.SPT: decision layer up front, then all 44 ascending by click count, ties broken by document order
policies.LPT: decision layer up front, then all 44 descending by click count, ties broken by document order
policies.LAZY-DOC: ungated block first in document order with NO upfront layer, then the decision layer (paid at the gated boundary), then the gated block in document order
policies.LAZY-SPT: ungated block ascending by click count (ties by document order), then the decision layer, then the gated block ascending (ties by document order)

metrics.clock: one owner-interaction unit per checkbox click and per decision reply; sequential service; progress retained; C_i = clock at sequence i's last click
metrics.live_curve: L_pi(t) = titles live after t interactions, t = 0..T (T = 263 BATCHED / 281 PER-ITEM)
metrics.TTF: min C_i (interactions to first live title)
metrics.T22: interactions to 22 live (the clock of the 22nd completion — the median title)
metrics.V_gamma: sum_i gamma^{C_i}, exact Fraction (attention-discounted live-title value; walk-away probability 1 - gamma per interaction)

decision_comparisons.gapMEAN: MEAN(DOC) - min MEAN over {SPT, LPT, LAZY-DOC, LAZY-SPT}, same accounting
decision_comparisons.ratioV: max V_gamma over the same four, divided by V_gamma(DOC), same accounting
decision_comparisons.TTF_ratio: TTF(DOC) against the family-best (min) TTF over the same four, same accounting

NULL_axes: order-insensitive (A1–A3 pass but A4 or A5 fails)
band straddle (gapMEAN(BATCHED) in (8, 10) or the TTF ratio in (2, 3))
gamma-conditional (a reporting gamma leg lands ratioV on the other side of 11/10 — named, never flipping the decision cell)
twin-arm disagreement surviving the INVALID diagnosis

gates.F1: data identity: the fixture's 44 rows recompute to the committed roll-ups exactly — 28 + 16 rows, sums 152 + 110 = 262, 16 gated flags, 19 decisions; all four numbers equal the committed current-state sentence quoted in this fixture
gates.F2: conservation: every policy's final clock = 262 + layer cost (263 BATCHED / 281 PER-ITEM) exactly; every policy ends at 44 live; every live curve is nondecreasing and steps by exactly 1 at each completion
gates.F3: sequencing theorems (exact): (a) EXCHANGE — all 43 adjacent transpositions of the SPT order weakly increase MEAN (each swap's MEAN effect is (n_j - n_i)/44 >= 0, asserted exactly) and weakly decrease V_gamma at every grid gamma; (b) POINTWISE DOMINANCE — L_SPT(t) >= L_DOC(t) and L_SPT(t) >= L_LPT(t) for EVERY t (same-layer family), and L_LAZY-SPT(t) >= L_LAZY-DOC(t) for every t; (c) V-anchor — V_gamma(SPT) > V_gamma(LPT) at every grid gamma in both accountings

F4_hand_world.note_disclosed: the registered hand-world 'LAZY' row is LAZY-SPT (its by-hand clocks (1, 3, 7) are the ungated-ascending deferral: 1-click at 1, 2-click at 3, layer at 4, gated 3-click at 7; LAZY-DOC on the same rows gives (2, 3, 7)); the sim runs all five policies on the hand world through both arms and gates the three registered rows

gates.F5: gamma-monotonicity: V_gamma(pi) strictly increasing in gamma over the grid for every policy, both accountings
gates.F6: battery: Arm B reproduces every Arm-A published number exactly; twin independently-written decision evaluators agree on the ruling token; Arm-R draw-count sentinels (one pmf uniform per sitting, counted and asserted per leg); aux seed 20261388 never read by any leg; stdout + results.json byte-identical across two process runs; Arms A/B platform-independent exact arithmetic, Arm R pinned to the stated CPython minor

reporting_legs.batch_door_price: per policy at the SAME order: MEAN(PER-ITEM) - MEAN(BATCHED) and TTF(PER-ITEM)/TTF(BATCHED); headline row DOC (the same-order cost of forgoing the one-reply defaults path)
reporting_legs.LPT_anchor: MEAN(LPT, BATCHED) — the maximally bad sort's anchor row
reporting_legs.gamma_sweep: ratioV at gamma in {49/50, 199/200} both accountings; the disclosed live edge: ratioV(BATCHED, 49/50) expected >= 11/10 — REPORTING only, the decision cell stays 99/100
reporting_legs.v020_exclusion: drop the 3-click 'V020 Probe (audience separation)' row (document-order row 44); re-evaluate every decision clause A1–A5 / R1–R3 on the 43-row world (MEAN denominator 43; same policy definitions, bands, and comparisons) — reporting-only, expected clause-by-clause identical booleans
reporting_legs.arm_r_reports: per policy at the decision cell (BATCHED): mean sittings-to-first-live, mean titles live after {1, 3, 10} sittings, total sittings and draw counts; trace-vs-exact-curve mismatch count (a drift is a named finding, never a ruling)

arm_r.draw_order_pinned: policies in canonical order, episodes sequential; ONE uniform per sitting inverted through the pinned pmf cdf in ascending budget order (3, 8, 21) with strict Fraction(u) < cdf comparisons; an episode runs sittings until all T = 263 interactions are spent (the last sitting may be partially used); draw sentinel: total uniforms == total sittings per leg
arm_r.no_statistical_gate: a trace drift from the exact curves is a named finding, never a ruling; sole gates = draw-count sentinel + aux-never-read + byte reproducibility

conventions_disclosed_before_runner.C1: document order = the 44-row order in rows_document_order above (ungated block then gated block, the committed section order); all tie-breaks read this index
conventions_disclosed_before_runner.C2: a sequence completes (its title goes live) when its last click is spent; the decision layer is NOT a title and never increments the live curve; no two sequences can complete on the same tick (min click count 3 >= 1), so every live-curve step is exactly 1
conventions_disclosed_before_runner.C3: Arm A (DECISION, seedless): schedule = layer block + sequence order per policy; completion clocks by integer prefix sums; MEAN and V_gamma exact fractions.Fraction; live curves materialized as integer arrays over t = 0..T
conventions_disclosed_before_runner.C4: Arm B (twin, seedless, independently written): an event-walk stepping the owner clock ONE interaction at a time — decrement the current item's remaining count, record completions and the live curve tick-by-tick, insert the decision layer where the policy places it; builds its own policy orders by explicit selection loops (no shared order-construction, prefix-sum, or metric helpers with Arm A); computes its own TTF/T22/MEAN/V_gamma from its walk
conventions_disclosed_before_runner.C5: twin decision evaluators: evaluator 1 reads Arm-A numbers, evaluator 2 independently re-reads Arm-B numbers with separately-written band logic; both emit a token; F6 asserts token equality
conventions_disclosed_before_runner.C6: Arm R grammar: chunked owner-sitting walk of the policy's own item list (its OWN third implementation — reading neither arm's curves for the trace): per sitting, spend min(budget, remaining) interactions consuming items in schedule order; record the sitting index of first completion, titles live after sittings 1/3/10, and total sittings; beside the trace, the same episode's cumulative interaction marks are read off the exact Arm-A curve and mismatches counted (reporting-only)
conventions_disclosed_before_runner.C7: canonical lowest-terms p/q strings for every exact value; float renderings via repr(float(Fraction)); results.json = json.dump(indent=1, sort_keys=True) + trailing newline, newline='\n'
conventions_disclosed_before_runner.C8: T22 = clock of the 22nd completion (22 = the registered median-title index of 44)
conventions_disclosed_before_runner.C9: presentation shuffle (seed 20261387) scope: row order of the ONE stdout presentation-shuffled policy-by-accounting summary listing only; results.json and all other stdout tables are canonical order
conventions_disclosed_before_runner.C10: the V020-exclusion leg rebuilds all five policy schedules on the 43-row world from scratch (the SPT/LPT ranks re-derive; the layer and gate semantics unchanged) and re-lands each clause boolean; it does NOT touch the main-world decision
conventions_disclosed_before_runner.C11: gapMEAN and ratioV and TTF ratios compare DOC to the best non-DOC family member in the SAME accounting, per the registered definitions; 'best' = min for MEAN and TTF, max for V_gamma
conventions_disclosed_before_runner.C12: F3 exchange transpositions are evaluated in BOTH accountings (the layer offset preserves the inequality; asserted anyway); the pointwise-dominance families are same-layer as registered: {DOC, SPT, LPT} upfront-layer and {LAZY-DOC, LAZY-SPT} deferred-layer, each checked per accounting

drafter_disclosed_never_gated.note: drafting arithmetic disclosed in the PROPOSAL 062 block per the P048–P061 closed-form-arm norm; the sim re-derives everything from scratch and never gates on these
